title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out with left Achilles tendinitis for the Celtics' game against the Atlanta Hawks.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla described Brown's condition as 'day-to-day' following a 31-point game against the Thunder.
Nikola Vucevic is also out with a finger fracture, while Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available for the game.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington, Massachusetts, have phased out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
A Boston.com poll showed 73% of readers oppose the ban, citing practicality issues, especially for larger yards.
Supporters of the ban highlight noise, air pollution, and health issues as key reasons for their stance.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts wastewater samples show the presence of the new BA.3.2 COVID-19 variant, nicknamed the 'cicada' variant.
BA.3.2, originating from South Africa in 2024, has reached 23 countries and is now in 132 samples in the U.S.
Current vaccines may be less effective against BA.3.2, but WHO suggests it poses low additional risk with no rise in severe cases.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that two DCF workers, Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski, may face trial over child removal.
Josh Sabey and Sarah Perkins allege DCF workers violated their rights by seizing their children without evidence of abuse.
The couple claims they were coerced into consenting to the removal, believing a SWAT team might be called.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's on Beacon Hill, led by Jamie Bissonnette, offers global-influenced pizzas and pastas.
Ladyfish in Portland, opening May 1, features Maine seafood with Chinese and Italian influences.
La Tavernetta in East Boston will provide coastal dining with oysters and spritzes this spring.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's novel "The Astral Library" features the Boston Public Library as a magical portal to fictional worlds.
"The Astral Library," a fantasy novel, diverges from Quinn's typical historical fiction works like "The Rose Code."
Quinn, a BU alum, draws from her Boston experiences, setting her book's character amidst familiar Boston locales like Newbury Street.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Councilors are urging police to update policies for releasing bodycam footage after a Roxbury police shooting.
The City Council passed a resolution with 9-3 support, led by Councilors Culpepper and Worrell, for greater transparency.
Prosecutors charged Officer Nicholas O’Malley with manslaughter in the March 11 shooting; bodycam footage remains unreleased.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opens at Six Flags New England on April 11.
The coaster accelerates riders to 45 mph, features 11 airtime moments, and is 2,604 feet long.
Quantum Accelerator is Six Flags New England's twelfth rollercoaster and the first new coaster since 2017.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Schools to introduce AI education program in fall 2026 for all high school students.
The program, with a $1 million grant from Paul English, will train teachers in AI curriculum.
Curriculum developed with UMass Boston focuses on ethical AI use and student creativity enhancement.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing after initially misnaming his brother as Sandra Birchmore's unborn child's father.
The Boston Globe incorrectly reported that William Farwell was the father, leading to confusion over a court filing footnote.
DNA testing showed neither Matthew nor William Farwell fathered Birchmore's child; the actual father remains unidentified.
